_provenance.extraction_method: Playwright Chromium 1440x900 @2x, networkidle + 1500ms, evaluated computed styles on :root, body, headings, paras, italics, CTAs, sections, logo candidates, meta, nav
synthesized_inputs: brand.mission — lightly reworded from meta description (kept factual)
voice.examples.dont — composed to contrast extracted voice rules; not read from the page
personas — derived from nav IA (GET HELP audience vs GIVE/GET INVOLVED audience); not explicitly named on site
photography.rules — inferred from the single hero image observed; full page photography library not yet crawled
motifs descriptions — observed from the hero screenshot and CTA styles
contentPillars.descriptions — inferred from nav + hero copy

palette_source.description_used: The Road Home's actual site palette — teal family, white, black, with neutral grays
palette_source.notes: Site is WordPress/WPBakery. Root contains WP preset colors (accent pink #CD2653, cream #F5EFE0) that are theme defaults, NOT actively used in rendered UI. The real brand palette is the teal family plus white/black, observed in CTAs, sections, and footer.

brand.name: The Road Home
brand.mission: Help people experiencing homelessness step out of homelessness through emergency shelter, supportive services, and housing assistance.
brand.anniversary_note: 100-year anniversary mark visible in the logo (centennial logo in use since 2023)
brand.positioning: A century-old frontline homeless services organization. The site is designed as a triage surface: one lane for people in crisis, one lane for donors and volunteers. Everything else is secondary.

logo.description: Centennial lockup: a small human-silhouette icon inside a doorway mark, beside a '100' numeral with 'THE ROAD HOME' wordmark underscored in teal. Multi-color but dominant teal.

primary.name: Road Home Teal
primary.use: Primary CTA fill (DONATE), active link accents, wordmark underscore

primary.name: Shelter Teal
primary.use: Deep teal footer, section grounds, inverted surfaces

primary.name: Ink
primary.use: Body copy on light grounds, inverted CTA text

primary.name: Paper
primary.use: Primary ground, display type over photography, inverted buttons

display.case: UPPERCASE for big display, Title Case for sub
display.notes: Heavy 900 weight at 45–60px for hero/section titles. Confident, institutional, not decorative. Consider a modern substitute for a redesign: Neue Haas Grotesk Display, Söhne Breit, or Inter Display 900.

body.notes: DIN Next is the German-industrial sibling of the display face. Functional, workmanlike, legible at small sizes. Modern substitutes: Inter, DM Sans, IBM Plex Sans.

typography.pairing_logic: A utility-first pair — both sans-serif, both engineered, no editorial italic accents. Holds a 'we show up and do the work' tone. Any redesign that swaps the faces should preserve this functional-not-precious register.

borderRadius.notes: Squared-off corners on buttons, inputs, cards. Reads as disciplined and public-sector.

primary.notes: Chunky teal slab. The principal visual emphasis on the page.

ghost.notes: Used for quick-nav items on dark/photo grounds.

rules: Real people in real settings — not stock pity imagery.
Subjects are dignified, composed, clothed for the weather they live in. Jackets, beanies, warmth.
Architectural or urban backgrounds — recognizably Salt Lake City.
Natural light, muted cool tones that let the teal brand color stay the loudest color on the page.
Room left for a centered white display headline overlay.
do: urban backdrop that contextualizes the mission
dont: Faces of clients shown in distress or shame
Stock photography that could be any nonprofit

rules: Lead with the action a reader can take right now (call this number, find a shelter).
Use uppercase display for slogans only. Do not shout in body copy.
Prefer triplets: 'Refuge. Resources. Relief.' — three-beat cadence for headlines.
State scale in concrete numbers: 'over 1,550 people in housing, in the community'.
Two distinct audiences, two distinct tones: to someone in crisis, be instructional; to donors, be brisk and accountable.

do: Our team may be able to help you find the resources you need: 801-359-2444
On any given night, we have over 1,550 people in housing, in the community.
The Road Home provides emergency shelter, supportive services, and housing assistance that help individuals and families step out of homelessness.
dont: Join us on our journey to end the crisis of unhoused communities.
Together, we can make a difference in the lives of those less fortunate.
Make an impact today by donating to our important cause.

motifs.name: Triplet display headline
motifs.description: Three one-word sentences separated by periods, uppercase, centered, white on photography.
motifs.usage: Hero and major section openers. 'REFUGE. RESOURCES. RELIEF.' is the canonical example.

motifs.name: Teal slab CTA
motifs.description: Squared-off, heavy-weight uppercase button in Road Home Teal (#008192) with generous horizontal padding.
motifs.usage: The DONATE button — the single consistent visual anchor across every page.

motifs.name: Full-width dignified portrait
motifs.description: Real subject, full-bleed, city as backdrop, headline overlay.
motifs.usage: Hero section of landing and program pages.

motifs.name: Centennial mark
motifs.description: The 100 numeral paired with the house-doorway icon and wordmark — currently the header lockup.
motifs.usage: Header and footer. Signals longevity and trust.

motifs.name: Phone-number-as-CTA
motifs.description: Emergency phone numbers (211, 801-990-9999, 801-359-2444) treated as primary affordances, not decorative.

personas.name: Person in crisis
personas.needs: Fastest path to shelter or a human on the phone. Low reading load, high contrast, phone-forward.

personas.name: Donor
personas.motto: I want to know my money works.
personas.needs: Concrete impact numbers, transparent 'Where Does It Go?', named giving paths (recurring, employer matching, legacy).

personas.name: Volunteer / advocate
personas.needs: Scheduled opportunities, program context, a way to bring friends/coworkers.

personas.name: Policy / partner
personas.motto: I need the org's data and credibility cues.
personas.needs: Annual reports, board, press, charity ratings.

contentPillars.name: Get Help
contentPillars.description: Crisis-first services: emergency shelter, supportive services, housing assistance. Audience: people experiencing or at risk of homelessness.

contentPillars.name: Give
contentPillars.description: Donation pathways: one-time, recurring (Housing Champions), employer matching, item donations, legacy/planned giving, real property, trusts.

contentPillars.name: Get Involved
contentPillars.description: Volunteer, fundraise, advocate. Audience: community members and corporate partners.

contentPillars.name: About
contentPillars.description: Who we are, our 100-year history, leadership, accountability, charity ratings (4-star).

contentPillars.name: Impact
contentPillars.description: Numbers, stories, annual reporting. 'On any given night, 1,550+ people housed.'
